qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Unauthenticated remote command execution via PTY WebSocket endpoint
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The advisory describes XSS (CWE-79) through the markdown renderer and URL override feature (?url= parameter in packages/app/src/app.tsx) as the root cause, with /pty/ endpoints as the downstream impact mechanism. The finding identifies missing authentication on /pty/ endpoints as the root cause (CWE-306). These are different vulnerability classes with different root causes: the advisory's vulnerable code is the unsanitized markdown renderer and CSP absence, while the finding's vulnerable code is the /pty/ endpoint handlers. The finding does not mention XSS, DOMPurify, CSP, the markdown renderer, or the ?url= parameter — the actual advisory root cause.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: The advisory (GHSA-c83v-7274-4vgp, CVE-2026-22813) describes an XSS vulnerability (CWE-79) in the OpenCode web UI caused by unsanitized HTML injection in the markdown renderer and the server URL override feature (?url= parameter in packages/app/src/app.tsx). The attack chain is: malicious website tricks user into loading a URL with ?url= parameter → XSS on localhost:4096 → same-origin fetch to /pty/ API → command execution. The sole qualifying finding (unauthenticated-pty-rce) identifies missing authentication on /pty/ endpoints as the root cause, which is a different vulnerability (CWE-306) with different vulnerable code (server.ts /pty/ route handlers, pty/index.ts PTY session creation). The finding does not address the advisory's root cause: the lack of DOMPurify sanitization and CSP in the web UI markdown renderer.
evidence: Advisory CWE is CWE-79 (Cross-site Scripting); finding vulnerability_type is unauthenticated_rce
Advisory root cause code: markdown renderer (no DOMPurify, no CSP) and ?url= parameter in packages/app/src/app.tsx
Finding root cause code: POST /pty and GET /pty/:ptyID/connect in packages/opencode/src/server/server.ts, PTY creation in packages/opencode/src/pty/index.ts
Finding does not mention XSS, markdown renderer, DOMPurify, CSP, or the ?url= parameter override feature
Advisory attack chain requires user interaction (UI:P) to trigger XSS; finding describes direct API access without user interaction (UI:N) — fundamentally different attack vectors
